title: Assurance & governance
intro: Benchmarks, testing, observability, and governance mechanisms that ensure AI systems operate responsibly and reliably.

items.name: MMLU
items.description: General-purpose benchmark designed to assess language understanding and reasoning across academic domains.
features: Multiple-choice questions spanning diverse subject areas.
Commonly used to compare general reasoning capabilities.
Frequently cited in model release reports.
items.usage: Baseline comparison of general model capability.

items.name: HELM
items.description: Benchmark suite developed to evaluate language models across multiple dimensions, including accuracy and robustness.
features: Covers a wide range of tasks and scenarios.
items.usage: Comparative analysis of trade-offs between models.

items.name: MTEB
items.description: Standardized benchmark for evaluating text embedding models across retrieval and clustering tasks.
features: Broad coverage of downstream embedding use cases.
Widely adopted for comparing embedding models.
items.usage: Selection and comparison of embedding models.

items.name: BEIR
items.description: Benchmark suite for evaluating information retrieval systems across heterogeneous datasets.
features: Focus on retrieval performance independent of generation.
Common reference for RAG system design.
items.usage: Evaluation of retrievers and vector search configurations.

items.name: LongBench
items.description: Benchmark designed to evaluate model performance on tasks requiring long-context understanding.
features: Tasks that stress context length and information retention.
Highlights degradation patterns as context grows.

items.name: RAGBench
items.description: Benchmark focused on evaluating RAG systems as combined pipelines.
features: Joint evaluation of retrieval and generation quality.
Metrics related to grounding and answer relevance.
Designed to reflect realistic RAG workflows.
items.usage: Comparative analysis of RAG pipeline designs.

items.name: Ragas
items.description: Open-source framework designed to evaluate Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines.
features: Metrics including faithfulness, answer relevance, and context recall.
Supports synthetic test data generation to jumpstart evaluation.
Integration with major LLM frameworks and observability tools.

items.name: DeepEval
items.description: Open-source evaluation framework for LLMs that provides a variety of metrics and testing workflows.
features: Support for unit testing LLM outputs with many built-in metrics.
Integrated dashboard for tracking evaluation results over time.
Enables the use of LLM-as-a-judge for complex quality assessments.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; paid cloud offering for enterprise teams.

items.name: Arize Phoenix
items.description: Open-source tool for LLM observability and evaluation that focuses on troubleshooting and improvement.
features: Visualizes embeddings to identify clusters of poor performance.
Automated evaluation of retrieval and generation quality.
Supports tracing and dataset versioning for reproducible testing.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; managed enterprise plans available.

items.name: Datadog
items.description: General-purpose observability platform extended to monitor AI-enabled services and infrastructure.
features: Monitoring of latency and resource usage for AI workloads.

items.name: Langfuse
items.description: Open-source observability platform designed for LLM applications.
features: End-to-end tracing of LLM requests and tool calls.
Prompt and context inspection with version awareness.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; paid plans for managed deployments.

items.name: Traceloop OpenLLMetry
items.description: Open-source instrumentation framework that brings LLM observability into the OpenTelemetry ecosystem.
features: Standardized tracing of LLM calls and prompts.

items.name: LangSmith
items.description: Observability and debugging platform integrated with the LangChain ecosystem.
features: Execution traces for chains, agents, and graphs.
Inspection of intermediate steps and tool interactions.
items.pricing: Free tier for developers; paid plans for teams and higher usage.

items.name: MLflow
items.description: Open-source platform for managing the AI lifecycle, including experiment tracking and model management.
features: Experiment tracking for logging parameters and metrics.
Model packaging and registry for versioning.
Support for evaluation and monitoring of AI applications.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; managed enterprise offerings available.

items.name: Microsoft Presidio
items.description: Toolkit for detecting and anonymizing sensitive information in text and images.

items.name: OpenDP
items.description: Framework for implementing differential privacy in data analysis.

items.name: OneTrust
items.description: Enterprise platform for privacy and regulatory compliance.

items.name: Apache Ranger
items.description: Framework for centralized data access control and auditing.

items.name: OpenAI Moderation
items.description: API-based content moderation service for detecting policy-violating inputs and outputs.
features: Classification of content across safety categories.
Real-time moderation of prompts and model responses.
items.pricing: Usage-based pricing as part of OpenAI API services.

items.name: Lakera
items.description: Security platform focused on protecting LLM applications against prompt injection.
features: Detection of prompt injection and jailbreak attempts.
Monitoring of tool calls and external integrations.

items.name: OWASP GenAI Security Project
items.description: Community-driven initiative providing guidelines for securing generative AI systems.

items.name: TextAttack
items.description: Open-source framework for adversarial testing and robustness evaluation of LLM models.
features: Support for attack and defense experimentation.

items.name: Guardrails AI
items.description: Framework for enforcing structural and safety constraints on LLM inputs and outputs.
features: Output validation using schemas and guardrail definitions.
Integration with LLM pipelines to enforce policy checks.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; commercial offerings available.

items.name: Weights & Biases
items.description: Experiment tracking and observability platform for AI systems.
items.pricing: Free tier available; paid enterprise plans.

items.name: DVC
items.description: Data and model version control system designed for reproducible workflows.

items.name: Arize AI
items.description: Monitoring and evaluation platform for production AI systems.

items.name: Evidently AI
items.description: Open-source framework for evaluating and monitoring model performance.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; paid cloud offering available.

items.name: OpenLineage
items.description: Open standard for tracking data lineage across systems.

items.name: SHAP
items.description: Explainability library for interpreting model predictions.

items.name: Jaeger
items.description: Open-source distributed tracing system for monitoring microservices.

items.name: IBM AI Fairness 360
items.description: Open-source toolkit for detecting and mitigating bias in AI models.

items.name: Fairlearn
items.description: Toolkit for assessing and improving fairness in machine learning models.

items.name: What-If Tool
items.description: Interactive tool for exploring model behavior and fairness issues.

items.name: Aequitas
items.description: Open-source bias audit toolkit for classification systems.

items.name: Labelbox
items.description: Data annotation and evaluation platform enabling human review loops.

items.name: Scale AI
items.description: Enterprise data labeling platform supporting human oversight operations.
features: Reinforcement learning from human feedback pipelines.

items.name: ServiceNow
items.description: Enterprise workflow platform used to implement review and approval processes.

items.name: Label Studio
items.description: Open-source data labeling and review platform for building human-in-the-loop workflows.
features: Support for text and multimodal labeling.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; paid enterprise plans available.

items.name: Sentry
items.description: Application monitoring and error-tracking platform used to respond to runtime issues.
features: Issue triage and incident response workflows.
items.pricing: Free tier available; paid plans based on usage.

items.name: Spec Kit
items.description: Open-source CLI toolkit that structures software development around specifications.
features: Four-phase workflow including specification and implementation.
Agent-agnostic design compatible with multiple coding agents.
Version-controlled specification artifacts integrated with Git.

items.name: OpenSpec
items.description: Open-source framework that implements SDD as a structured workflow.
features: End-to-end workflow from proposal to implementation.
Native integration with multiple AI agents such as Cursor.

items.name: BMad Method (BMM)
items.description: SDD methodology that combines structured specifications with multi-agent workflows.
features: Role-based agent system for planning and implementation.
Emphasis on structured documentation and process discipline.

items.name: Kiro
items.description: IDE built around SDD that combines coding and planning within a unified environment.
features: Built-in support for specification-first workflows within the IDE.
Integrated agent execution and automation capabilities.
items.pricing: Free tier available; paid plans for advanced usage.

items.name: Tessl
items.description: Platform for building software systems where specifications are the primary artifact.
features: Treats specifications as executable artifacts that drive implementation.
AI-assisted code generation aligned with defined specifications.
Focus on maintaining consistency between intent and implementation.
